Title: Microbiome-wide association studies reveal correlations between the structure and metabolism of the rhizosphere microbiome and disease resistance in cassava

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ABC transporter involved in lantibiotic export
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker for identifying functional nisin-producing microbiomes for biocontrol application
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisT was quantified as a nisin metabolism-related gene in cassava rhizosphere metagenomes. 2. Its abundance was elevated in resistant cassava cultivars. 3. The nisin biosynthesis/transport module tracked with increased root nisin and resistance to Xam.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states nisT is involved in transport of nisin.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Post-translational modification module of lantibiotic biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dehydrates specific Ser/Thr residues in the nisin precursor peptide during nisin maturation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gene abundance significantly higher in resistant cultivar samples, especially R03
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/module for selecting or engineering beneficial Lactococcus-rich rhizosphere microbiomes to improve cassava bacterial blight resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisB was one of six nisin biosynthesis-associated genes tracked in cassava rhizosphere metagenomes. 2. Its relative abundance was higher in disease-resistant cassava cultivars, especially R03. 3. Increased abundance of nisin-related genes was linked to higher root nisin content and enhanced resistance to Xam.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene identified from UniProt database and analyzed as part of microbial metagenome; function description in paper cites modification role for nisB.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Post-translational cyclization module of lantibiotic biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes thioether ring formation in the modified nisin precursor peptide
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of the target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gene abundance significantly higher in resistant cultivar samples, especially R03
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/module for selecting or engineering beneficial Lactococcus-rich rhizosphere microbiomes to improve cassava bacterial blight resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisC was one of six nisin biosynthesis-associated genes enriched in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes. 2. Higher abundance of nisC tracked with higher nisin content in roots. 3. The associated nisin suppressed Xam growth and enhanced cassava defense.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gene identified from UniProt database and analyzed as part of microbial metagenome; function description in paper cites modification role for nisC.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Subtilisin-like serine protease involved in lantibiotic processing
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proteolytically removes the leader peptide from modified prenisin to release active nisin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gene abundance significantly higher in resistant cultivar samples, especially R03
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker for identifying nisin-producing microbial consortia associated with disease-resistant cassava
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisP abundance was tracked across rhizosphere metagenomes of ten cassava varieties. 2. Resistant varieties showed higher abundance of nisin-related genes including nisP. 3. These associations support active nisin production as part of microbiome-mediated disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly states nisP is involved in activation of nisin via processing.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NisRK two-component regulatory system response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes autoregulatory expression of nisin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports activation/autoregulation of nisin biosynthesis in Lactococcus associated with resistant cassava rhizospheres
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gene abundance significantly higher in resistant cultivar samples, especially R03
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory marker for nisin-producing beneficial microbiomes in cassava biocontrol
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisR was among six nisin-related genes enriched in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes. 2. The paper places nisR in the autoregulatory nisRK system. 3. Enrichment of nisR-associated nisin biosynthesis capacity correlates with disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct regulator-target binding assay was performed in this paper; role inferred from known nisRK function cited by authors.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NisRK two-component regulatory system sensor kinase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antibacterial activity against Xanthomonas axonopodis pv. manihotis and promotion of cassava disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Signal sensing and phosphorylation-mediated activation of response regulator
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: nisR and nisin biosynthesis operon via NisRK system
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes autoregulatory expression of nisin biosynthesis genes through two-component signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports activation/autoregulation of nisin biosynthesis in Lactococcus associated with resistant cassava rhizospheres
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher relative abundance in resistant cassava rhizosphere microbiomes is associated with higher nisin abundance and stronger disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gene abundance significantly higher in resistant cultivar samples, especially R03
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic abundance analysis; correlation with disease resistance; nisin quantification; in vitro antibacterial assay of nisin; in vivo nisin treatment assay
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Rhizosphere microbiome of field-grown cassava; Xam infection; exogenous nisin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory marker for deployment of nisin-producing Lactococcus-enriched microbiomes for cassava disease control
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: 16S rRNA amplicon sequencing; ITS sequencing; shotgun metagenomics (NCBI SRA: PRJNA565326); cassava resequencing
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nisK was one of the six nisin metabolism-related genes analyzed across cassava rhizosphere metagenomes. 2. Resistant varieties contained higher abundance of these genes. 3. The associated nisin-producing capacity correlated with improved resistance to Xam.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct signaling assay was performed in this paper; role inferred from known nisRK function cited by authors.
